name: HMSAS Natal (K 10)
events: 14 Mar 1945 German U-boat U-714 was sunk in the North Sea near the Firth of Forth, in position 55°57'N, 01°57'W, by depth charges from the South African frigate HMSAS Natal and the British destroyer HMS Wivern.

26 Mar 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Welcome (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W. Downing, RNVR), HMS Felicity (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.F. Harmer-Elliot, RNVR), HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) and HMS Trondra (T/Lt. W.R. Russell, RNVR).

Upon completion of these exercises O 23 proceeded to Loch Alsh. She joined HMS Turpin (A/Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) and HMS Viking (Lt. R. Bannar-Martin, DSC, RN) for the passage. HNoMS Molde was escorting them. (1)

7 Apr 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (2)

8 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (3)

9 Apr 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (2)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
